title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Go Client Systems · Independent Go Trial
summary: A backup automation team integrating an unfamiliar artifact service must satisfy changed endpoint, payload, authority, byte, and time constraints without a recipe for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Go 1.26 net/http spec-aware cache decision notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Private Shared Cache
steps.instruction:
lab case — Go backup artifact client stores a response with max-age=50, ETag "rev-5", Age:5, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Private Shared Cache.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Private Shared Cache, keep Private Shared Cache connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
